First rewrite the left‐hand side with the double–angle identity  

 cos²x − sin²x = cos(2x).

Then  
 cos²x − sin²x − 1 = 0  
 ⟹ cos(2x) − 1 = 0  
 ⟹ cos(2x) = 1.

The cosine equals 1 when its argument is an integer multiple of 2π:

 2x = 2kπ (k ∈ ℤ)  
 ⟹ x = kπ.

Within the interval 0 ≤ x < 2π, the integer k can be 0 or 1, giving

 x = 0, π.

Therefore the exact solutions on [0, 2π) are  

 x = 0 and x = π.
